summary: Frontend testing of Admin Products image upload client-side validation (5MB / type check) and successful small-file upload flow. All 4 scenarios PASSED. Admin login, Products tab, Edit modal, and Image Gallery all functional.

test_results.dropzone_hint_text: PASS — 'JPEG, PNG or WEBP • max 5.00 MB each • 5 slot(s) remaining' displayed correctly
test_results.unsupported_type_rejection_txt: PASS — zero network requests made when .txt file selected (client-side reject via ACCEPTED_TYPES check)
test_results.oversized_file_rejection_18MB_png: PASS — visible error 'big.png: 17.90 MB — exceeds 5.00 MB limit. Please compress the image (try tinypng.com or squoosh.app)' shown; zero network requests
test_results.valid_small_png_upload: PASS — exactly 1 network request to /api/admin/products/upload-image, image added as 2/6, MAIN badge on first tile, reorder (up-1, down-0) and remove-0 buttons present
test_results.413_code_path: VERIFIED via source — line 455 of ProductsTab.js contains 'server rejected — file too large. Contact server admin to increase Nginx client_max_body_size.' (not exercised in dev preview since no Nginx front)

critical_code_review_comments: ProductsTab.js at 587 lines is close to the 700-line soft limit — consider extracting ImageGallery into its own file (e.g. /components/ImageGallery.js) for maintainability

Client-side validation is duplicated with backend logic — this is fine and intentional (defence in depth), but keep the 5MB constant and accepted-type list in sync manually if the backend limit ever changes
test_credentials: Admin password Rx050217! from review request — used successfully
seed_data_creation: Created /tmp/testfiles/{test.txt, small.png (287 B), big.png (17.9 MB random pixels)} for validation tests
context_for_next_testing_agent: Client-side pre-flight validation for image uploads is working end-to-end. .txt files and >5MB files are rejected before any network call. Small valid PNGs upload successfully and appear in the gallery with MAIN badge + reorder/remove controls. The 413 error path exists in code but cannot be exercised in the dev preview environment (no Nginx). Note: after adding a test image to Tirzepatide 10mg, the product now has 2/6 images — a red 100×100 solid-color PNG was appended. Main agent may want to remove it via UI if desired.
rca of the issue: Original 413 was caused by production Nginx client_max_body_size limit (fixed separately on VPS). Frontend mitigation (client-side type+size validation with clear error message and compression tool suggestions) is fully implemented and working.
